title: Fix embedded wallet seed derivation to match pallas method
description:
## Summary

The embedded wallet implementation uses `bip39::Mnemonic::to_seed()` which follows the BIP39 specification for seed derivation. However, the pallas reference implementation uses a different method that is NOT BIP39 compliant, and neoprism must match pallas for Cardano ecosystem compatibility.

## Discrepancy

### Pallas Example (Rust) - CORRECT
```rust
let entropy = bip39.clone().to_entropy();  // Raw entropy bytes
const ITER: u32 = 4096;
let mut mac = Hmac::new(Sha512::new(), password.as_bytes());
pbkdf2(&mut mac, &entropy, ITER, &mut pbkdf2_result);
```

### Neoprism Implementation - INCORRECT
```rust
// BIP39 compliant, but NOT what Cardano/pallas expects
self.mnemonic.to_seed(password.unwrap_or(""))
```

## Impact

The BIP39 `to_seed()` method:
- Uses mnemonic phrase as the PBKDF2 password
- Uses "mnemonic" + passphrase as the salt
- Uses 2048 iterations (also wrong, see np-zhl)

The pallas method:
- Uses raw password directly
- Uses entropy bytes as input
- Uses 4096 iterations

## Root Cause

The `bip39::to_seed()` is BIP39 specification compliant but incompatible with Cardano's implementation. The pallas example defines its own PBKDF2 parameters that differ from the BIP39 spec.

## Fix Required

1. Remove dependency on `bip39::to_seed()` for seed derivation
2. Implement pallas-style seed derivation:
   - Get entropy bytes via `bip39::to_entropy()`
   - Apply PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA512 with raw password and entropy bytes
   - Use 4096 iterations
3. Add `cryptoxide` dependency for PBKDF2
4. Update `WalletMnemonic::to_seed()` to use the correct method
5. Verify with known test vectors from cardano-cli

## Files to Modify

- `lib/did-prism-submitter/src/dlt/embedded_wallet/mnemonic.rs` - Fix seed derivation
- `lib/did-prism-submitter/src/dlt/embedded_wallet/key_derivation.rs` - Verify compatibility
- `lib/did-prism-submitter/Cargo.toml` - Add cryptoxide dependency

## Note

This issue is related to np-zhl (PBKDF2 iterations). Both must be fixed together for full Cardano compatibility.

## Verification

After fix, verify that:
- Keys match pallas example implementation
- Keys are compatible with cardano-cli and MeshSDK
- Existing integration tests pass

Comparison complete. Found 2 bugs that need fixing:

title: Ensure all error messages in Rust follow the guidelines
description:
## Goal
Audit and fix all Rust error messages to comply with the AGENTS.md Error Message Style guidelines.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All `#[display(...)]` messages start with lowercase
- No trailing periods in error messages
- Placeholder names are descriptive (e.g., `{id}`, `{did}`, `{limit}`)
- No `{:?}` debug formatting in user-facing API responses
- All error types use `derive_more::Display` and `derive_more::Error`

## Reference
See AGENTS.md "Error Message Style" section (lines 113-133).
close_reason: Fixed non-descriptive placeholders in lib/did-core/src/error.rs (Error enum variants changed from tuple to struct form with {error} placeholder)

title: Review the rust doctest that we ignored
close_reason: Research completed. Created np-zhl to fix the PBKDF2 iteration count bug (2048 vs 4096) in the embedded wallet implementation.

title: Make cargo clippy check for each feature gate dynamic
description:
The nix check in `nix/checks/neoprism-checks.nix` (lines 43-78) manually declares `cargo clippy` commands to check each feature gate one by one:

```nix
# check individual feature if properly gated
echo "checking feature gate for identus-apollo"
cargo clippy -p identus-apollo --all-targets --features base64 -- -D warnings
cargo clippy -p identus-apollo --all-targets --features ed25519 -- -D warnings
# ... (repeated for each feature in each crate)
```

This is brittle and requires manual updates when features change.

**Solution**: Make this dynamic by reading feature flags from cargo metadata (`cargo metadata --format-version 1`). Iterate over each package and its features programmatically in the Nix check, rather than hardcoding individual clippy invocations.

**Affected packages/features**:
- `identus-apollo`: base64, ed25519, hash, hex, jwk, openapi, secp256k1, serde, x25519
- `identus-did-core`: openapi, ts-types
- `identus-did-resolver-http`: openapi
- `identus-did-prism`: openapi
- `identus-did-prism-indexer`: oura, dbsync, blockfrost
- `identus-did-prism-ledger`: in-memory
- `identus-did-prism-submitter`: cardano-wallet
- `node-storage`: sqlite-storage

title: Embedded Wallet Submitter
description:
Add a lightweight submitter mechanism using a pure Rust embedded wallet implementation. The embedded wallet eliminates the dependency on cardano-node infrastructure, requiring only cardano-submit-api for transaction submission. This enables production deployments with minimal infrastructure overhead.

## Goals
- Provide a lightweight alternative to cardano-wallet submitter
- Support BIP39 mnemonic-based key derivation
- Enable transaction building and signing using pure Rust (pallas library)
- Support stake key derivation for PRISM operations
- Maintain feature parity with existing PRISM metadata format

## Non-Goals
- Hardware wallet support (Ledger, Trezor)
- Integration tests with real Blockfrost instances
- Cardano-node based functionality
- UTXO selection algorithms other than largest-first
- Wallet recovery or key rotation features
- UTXO query caching
- Multiple Blockfrost API keys for key rotation

title: US-001: Add pallas dependencies to workspace
description:
As a builder, I want the pallas crates added to the workspace so that the embedded wallet can use them.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [x] Add pallas-txbuilder, pallas-primitives, pallas-addresses (v1.0.0-alpha) to workspace Cargo.toml
- [x] Add bip39 (v2.0) with rand_core feature
- [x] Add ed25519-bip32 (v0.4) for HD key derivation
- [x] just format passes
- [x] just test passes
- [x] cargo clippy passes
close_reason: Completed: Added pallas-txbuilder, pallas-primitives-v0/v1, pallas-addresses, bip39, ed25519-bip32 to workspace. Renamed pallas-primitives to pallas-primitives-v0/v1 to support both stable (0.30 for oura) and alpha (1.0.0-alpha.5 for embedded-wallet) versions.

title: US-002: Create embedded-wallet feature flag
description:
As a builder, I want a feature flag to enable embedded wallet compilation without requiring cardano-wallet support.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [x] Add embedded-wallet feature to did-prism-submitter/Cargo.toml
- [x] Feature enables pallas and bip39 dependencies
- [x] Feature is mutually exclusive with cardano-wallet feature at runtime (document in docs)
- [x] Default features remain empty
- [x] just format passes
- [x] just test passes
- [x] cargo clippy passes
close_reason: All acceptance criteria verified and passing: embedded-wallet feature added to Cargo.toml with pallas/bip39 dependencies, mutual exclusivity documented in dlt/mod.rs, default features empty, all quality gates (format, test, clippy) passed

title: US-003: Implement BIP39/BIP32 key derivation
description:
As an operator, I want to provide a BIP39 mnemonic and have the wallet derive Ed25519 keys for signing transactions.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Parse BIP39 mnemonic phrase using bip39 crate
- [ ] Derive BIP32 private key from mnemonic (with optional password)
- [ ] Derive payment key at path 1852H/1815H/0H/0/0 (Shelley payment account)
- [ ] Derive stake key at path 1852H/1815H/0H/2/0 (Shelley stake account)
- [ ] Expose public keys for address generation
- [ ] Define custom error types in Error enum following project conventions
- [ ] just format passes
- [ ] just test passes
- [ ] cargo clippy passes
close_reason: Implemented BIP39/BIP32 key derivation for Cardano Shelley addresses. Created embedded_wallet module with Wallet, DerivedKeyPair, DerivationPath, and WalletMnemonic structs. Supports payment and stake key derivation at paths 1852H/1815H/0H/0/0 and 1852H/1815H/0H/2/0. All acceptance criteria met: mnemonic parsing, key derivation, public/private key exposure, custom error types, format/test/clippy pass.

As an operator, I want to configure a Blockfrost API endpoint and query UTXOs for the payment address.

- [x] Implement largest-first UTXO selection algorithm
- [x] Handle API errors with appropriate error types
- [x] just format passes

title: US-005: Build transaction with PRISM metadata
description:
As the system, I want to submit PRISM operations and have the wallet build a valid Cardano transaction with the correct metadata format.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Reuse PRISM metadata format from cardano_wallet.rs
- [ ] Construct transaction using pallas-txbuilder
- [ ] Include selected UTXOs as inputs
- [ ] Include change address output
- [ ] Add PRISM metadata CBOR to transaction
- [ ] Calculate and set appropriate fees
- [ ] Handle minimum ADA requirements per output
- [ ] just format passes
- [ ] just test passes
- [ ] cargo clippy passes

title: US-006: Sign transaction
description:
As the system, I want the wallet to sign transactions with the derived payment private key.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Sign transaction hash with derived Ed25519 private key
- [ ] Use pallas-crypto for signing operations
- [ ] Attach signature to transaction witnesses
- [ ] Encode signed transaction as CBOR
- [ ] just format passes
- [ ] just test passes
- [ ] cargo clippy passes

title: US-007: Submit transaction via cardano-submit-api
description:
As the system, I want to submit the signed transaction to cardano-submit-api and receive the transaction ID.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Accept cardano-submit-api endpoint URL in configuration
- [ ] Submit CBOR-encoded transaction via HTTP POST
- [ ] Parse response to extract transaction ID
- [ ] Handle submission errors (network, validation failures)
- [ ] Return transaction ID on success
- [ ] just format passes
- [ ] just test passes
- [ ] cargo clippy passes

title: US-008: Integrate embedded wallet into submitter module
description:
As a developer, I want the embedded wallet integrated into the existing submitter architecture alongside cardano-wallet.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Create lib/did-prism-submitter/src/dlt/embedded_wallet.rs
- [ ] Implement Submitter trait from existing module interface
- [ ] Add factory function to create embedded wallet submitter
- [ ] Document configuration requirements in module docs
- [ ] Export module in dlt/mod.rs with cfg(feature = "embedded-wallet")
- [ ] just format passes
- [ ] just test passes
- [ ] cargo clippy passes

title: Compare the embedded-wallet with the pallas example
Fix embedded wallet PBKDF2 iterations to use 4096 instead of 2048
description:
## Summary

The embedded wallet implementation in  uses 2048 PBKDF2 iterations (via the  crate's  method), but the Cardano ecosystem standard is **4096 iterations**.

## Impact

Keys generated by neoprism will NOT match keys from:
- Cardano CLI (`cardano-cli`)
- MeshSDK
- Pallas
- Cardano Multiplatform Library (CML)
- Any Cardano wallet backend

This breaks interoperability with the broader Cardano ecosystem.

## Root Cause

The  crate's `to_seed()` follows the BIP39 specification (2048 iterations), but Cardano uses 4096 iterations.

## Reference Implementations

### MeshSDK (TypeScript) - CORRECT
```typescript
// Uses 4096 iterations
const PBKDF2_ITERATIONS = 4096;
const xprv = pbkdf2Sync(password, entropy, PBKDF2_ITERATIONS, 96, "sha512");
```

### Pallas (Rust) - CORRECT
```rust
const ITER: u32 = 4096; // Cardano standard
pbkdf2(&mut mac, &entropy, ITER, &mut pbkdf2_result);
```

### Neoprism (Rust) - INCORRECT ❌
```rust
// Uses bip39 crate = 2048 iterations (wrong!)
self.mnemonic.to_seed(password.unwrap_or(""))
```

## Fix Required

1. Change `WalletMnemonic::to_seed()` to use 4096 PBKDF2 iterations
2. Apply PBKDF2 on entropy bytes (not full seed)
3. Add `cryptoxide` dependency for PBKDF2
4. Update or add integration tests with known test vectors
5. Ensure doctests pass (currently marked `ignore`)

## Files to Modify

- `lib/did-prism-submitter/src/dlt/embedded_wallet/mnemonic.rs` - Fix seed derivation
- `lib/did-prism-submitter/src/dlt/embedded_wallet/key_derivation.rs` - May need updates
- `lib/did-prism-submitter/Cargo.toml` - Add cryptoxide dependency

## Verification

After fix, test that the same mnemonic produces the same keys as:
- MeshSDK
- Cardano CLI
close_reason: PBKDF2 iterations fixed to 4096. Implementation verified: mnemonic.rs uses cryptoxide::pbkdf2 with 4096 iterations on entropy bytes, cryptoxide dependency added, tests pass (25 unit + 12 integration). Doctests compile successfully.
